1	# Extensions Boundary
2	
3	This directory contains bundled plugins. Treat it as the same boundary that
4	third-party plugins see.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
14	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
15	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md`
16	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
17	- Definition files:
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
22	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
23	  - `package.json`
24	
25	## Boundary Rules
26	
27	- Extension production code should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and its
28	  own local barrels such as `./api.ts` and `./runtime-api.ts`.
29	- Do not import core internals from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`,
30	  `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`, or another extension's `src/**`.
31	- Do not use relative imports that escape the current extension package root.
32	- Keep plugin metadata accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and the package
33	  `openclaw` block so discovery and setup work without executing plugin code.
34	- Treat files like `src/**`, `onboard.ts`, and other local helpers as private
35	  unless you intentionally promote them through `api.ts` and, if needed, a
36	  matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade.
37	- If core or core tests need a bundled plugin helper, export it from `api.ts`
38	  first instead of letting them deep-import extension internals.
39	
40	## Expanding The Boundary
41	
42	- If an extension needs a new seam, add a typed Plugin SDK subpath or additive
43	  export instead of reaching into core.
44	- Keep new plugin-facing seams backwards-compatible and versioned. Third-party
45	  plugins consume this surface.
46	- When intentionally expanding the contract, update the docs, exported subpath
47	  list, package exports, and API/contract checks in the same change.
48

## PulseBoard channel plugin — created at `extensions/pulseboard/`

**28 new files, zero existing files modified.**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest: `id=pulseboard`, `channels=["pulseboard"]` |

| `index.ts` | `defineChannelPluginEntry` — exports `pulseboardPlugin` and `setPulseboardRuntime` |
| `setup-entry.ts` | `defineSetupPluginEntry` for the setup wizard |

### Core source (`src/`)
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `types.ts` | `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `PulseBoardConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, `PulseBoardProbe` |
| `runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore` — `getPulseboardRuntime` / `setPulseboardRuntime` |
| `runtime-api.ts` | Internal SDK barrel re-exporting generic channel helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` (note: a `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` subpath would be added in a real landing) |
| `config-schema.ts` | Zod schemas: `PulseBoardBoardSchema`, `PulseBoardAccountSchema`, `PulseBoardConfigSchema`, `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema` |
| `config-ui-hints.ts` | `ChannelConfigUiHint` entries for every config field |
| `accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseboardAccount`, `listPulseboardAccountIds`, `listEnabledPulseboardAccounts` — env vars `PULSEBOARD_API_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_HANDLE` |
| `normalize.ts` | `normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget` (handles `!board`, `board:`, `user:`, `pulseboard:` prefixes), `isBoardTarget`, `normalizePulseboardAllowEntry`, `resolvePulseboardAllowlistMatch` |
| `policy.ts` | `resolvePulseboardBoardMatch`, `resolvePulseboardBoardAccessGate`, `resolvePulseboardRequireMention`, `resolvePulseboardMentionGate`, `resolvePulseboardBoardSenderAllowed` |
| `client.ts` | Fictional PulseBoard HTTP API client — `fetchPulseBoardMe`, `postToPulseBoard`, `sendPulseBoardDm`, `pollPulseBoardStream` (newline-delimited JSON long-poll) |
| `send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseboard` — routes `!board` targets via `postToPulseBoard`, handles via `sendPulseBoardDm` |
| `probe.ts` | `probePulseboard` — hits `/v1/me`, checks latency + handle match |
| `inbound.ts` | `handlePulseboardInbound` — full DM/board policy pipeline (pairing, allowlists, mention gate, command gate, `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`) |
| `monitor.ts` | `monitorPulseboardProvider` — long-poll loop with back-off, event→message conversion, own-message filtering |
| `setup-core.ts` | `pulseboardSetupAdapter`, `updatePulseboardAccountConfig`, `setPulseboardDmPolicy`, `setPulseboardAllowFrom`, `setPulseboardBoardAccess` |
| `setup-surface.ts` | `pulseboardSetupWizard` — full `ChannelSetupWizard` with env-detect, text inputs, groupAccess, allowFrom, finalize (mention prompt) |
| `channel.ts` | `pulseboardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin` — 4000-char chunk limit, direct+group chatTypes, status/probe/gateway/pairing/security/outbound wired up |

`normalize.test.ts`, `accounts.test.ts`, `policy.test.ts`, `send.test.ts`, `probe.test.ts`, `inbound.test.ts`, `monitor.test.ts`, `channel.test.ts`
